ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON OCT-25-2011 IN DEPT. 503, PLAINTIFF JERRY FULLMER'S MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING IS GRANTED PURSUANT CCP 36 (A) & (D). TRIAL IS SET FOR 2/6/12 AT 11:15 AM IN DEPARTMENT 503. MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT TO BE HEARD ON 15 DAYS NOTICE WITHOUT AN ADDITIONAL TWO DAYS FOR ESERVICE. LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTIONS IS 1/26/12. PLAINTIFFS TO MAKE A SETTLEMENT DEMAND ON ALL DEFENDANTS NO LATER THAN 1/9/12. SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IN ROOM 603 AT 1:30 PM ON 1/26/12. A CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET ON 11/28/11 AT 1:30 PM IN DEPT. 503. FACT DISCOVERY CUT OFF IS 1/13/12 AND EXPERT DISCOVERY CUT OFF IS 2/3/12. PLAINTIFF TO PROVIDE UPDATED MEDICAL AND RADIOLOGY TO DEFENDANTS BY 11/18/11. NO LATER THAN 11/18/11, PLAINTIFF TO PROVIDE A STATEMENT STATING AS TO EACH DEFENDANT THE NAME AND CONTACT INFORMATION OF EACH PERSON WITH KNOWLEDGE OF ANY FACTS SUPPORTING THE PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM AND WHAT KNOWLEDGE THERE IS. ANY PERSON NOT IDENTIFIED ON THAT STATEMENT WILL BE BARRED FROM TESTIFYING AT TRIAL OR PRESENTING A DECLARATION ON SUMMARY JUDGMENT ABSENT A SHOW OF GOOD CAUSE. DEFENDANTS SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL LAY WITNESSES THAT THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT LEARNED OF THROUGH ITS OWN DISCOVERY EFFORTS AND THAT THEY WILL RELY ON FOR PROPOSITION 51 PURPOSES OR FOR THEIR AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS UPON WHICH THESE WITNESSES RELY, AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY 1/9/12. PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE AN ORDER. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON; CLERK: E. BURA; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (503)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-26-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Gavin D. Whitis, Esq., of POND NORTH LLP (415-217-1240), appearing for defendant / moving party Western Auto Supply Company
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Jerry Fullmer

RULING:
DEFENDANT WESTERN AUTO SUPPLY COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Western Auto Supply Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of production under Aguilar. Defendant failed to negate an essential element of plaintiff's claim. Even if Mr. Wright's declaration is admissible, it does not demonstrate that the Western Auto store at issue was an independently owned dealer store that was not owned, operated or controlled by Western Auto. Further, even if paragraph 11 of Mr. Wright's declaration is admissible, it merely creates a triable issue when read in conjunction with plaintiff's deposition testimony. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Jennifer Seelenbacher, CSR #11878 = (503/TLJ)
